title: pinus brutia var. eldarica
intro: Pinus brutia var. eldarica, also known as the Turkish pine, is a variety of pine tree native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. The essential oil extracted from its needles has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The oil is rich in terpenes, which have been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the oil is often used in aromatherapy, skincare products, and traditional remedies, such as teas and infusions, to harness its potential health benefits.
